name: JFrog Xray Security & Compliance API
what_it_does: Software composition analysis and security scanning platform with REST API — enabling DevSecOps teams to scan artifacts stored in JFrog Artifactory for vulnerabilities (CVEs), license compliance issues, operational risk, and generate SBOMs, with deep integration into the JFrog Platform for supply chain security.
use_cases: Scanning artifacts via JFrog Xray API for CVE vulnerability detection in CI/CD pipelines
Generating SBOMs via JFrog Xray API for software supply chain transparency and compliance
Enforcing security policies via JFrog Xray API for blocking vulnerable artifacts from promotion
Checking license compliance via JFrog Xray API for open-source license risk management
Integrating security gates via JFrog Xray API in Jenkins/GitHub Actions for shift-left security
not_for: SAST/source code scanning (use Semgrep or CodeQL for source analysis)
Container runtime security (use Falco for runtime; Xray is pre-runtime scanning)
Teams not using JFrog Artifactory (Xray requires Artifactory as the artifact store)
best_when: A DevSecOps team already using JFrog Artifactory needs deep artifact-level security scanning tightly integrated with their existing artifact management, with SBOM generation and policy enforcement.
avoid_when: You're not on JFrog Artifactory, or need standalone SCA without the JFrog Platform dependency.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth (username:password) or Bearer token (user access token). Header: Authorization: Bearer {token} or Authorization: Basic {base64}. JFrog access tokens: generated in Artifactory/JFrog Platform identity. REST API at {artifactory-host}/xray/api/v3/. Self-hosted: your Artifactory server URL. JFrog SaaS: {your-server}.jfrog.io/xray/. Webhooks for scan completion and policy violations. JFrog CLI: primary automation interface.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier for Xray; JFrog Artifactory has free OSS tier (no Xray)
pricing.paid_tiers: Part of JFrog Platform: Enterprise+ plans; estimated $500-2000/month
pricing.notes: Israeli company (Netanya). Founded 2008 by Yoav Landman and Frederic Simon. IPO: NASDAQ (FROG) 2020. JFrog Platform: Artifactory (artifact management) + Xray (security) + Distribution + Pipelines. Xray requires JFrog Artifactory — cannot run standalone. Self-hosted or JFrog SaaS (cloud.jfrog.io). Database: JFrog maintains vulnerability database from NVD, GHSA, and proprietary research. Watch policies: policy sets with rules (minimum severity, license type, etc.) that block/alert/notify. SBOM: CycloneDX and SPDX format generation. Integration: GitHub, Jenkins, Azure DevOps, Kubernetes. JFrog CLI: jf scan for local scanning without full Artifactory.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; enterprise rate limits enforced

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Bearer or Basic auth. Requires JFrog Artifactory (not standalone). Self-hosted URL config required. JFrog CLI (jf) preferred for CI/CD. SBOM: CycloneDX/SPDX. Watch policies for enforcement. Israeli company. NASDAQ public. FedRAMP authorized.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Scan requests: idempotent for same artifact checksum. Policy reads idempotent. SBOM generation: idempotent by artifact version. Webhook events: implement idempotent handlers.
known_agent_gotchas: Requires JFrog Artifactory: Xray cannot scan artifacts outside Artifactory — artifacts must be stored there first
Self-hosted URL: API base is YOUR Artifactory server ({your-server}/xray/api/v3/) — not a fixed URL
JFrog access token: prefer over HTTP Basic — generate in JFrog Platform Identity with appropriate permissions
Watch policies: configure Watches and Policies in Xray UI before API-based enforcement works
JFrog CLI (jf): preferred for CI/CD scanning — handles token refresh and Xray configuration
SBOM generation: separate API endpoint (/sbom) — CycloneDX and SPDX formats available

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token (JFrog access token) preferred over Basic auth. SOC2/ISO27001/FedRAMP authorized. JFrog Platform is a trusted artifact management standard. SBOM generation for supply chain transparency. Israeli company: GDPR compliance via EU data residency. Self-hosted option: data never leaves your environment. JFrog CLI handles credential storage. Artifactory dependency: Xray security inherits Artifactory's posture. Watch policies: enforcement prevents vulnerable artifact promotion.
